title: Compare the serious adverse event profile across the three approved BTK inhibitors
subtitle: Grouped by event category and pivotal trial
description: An agent pulls the pivotal trials for ibrutinib, acalabrutinib, and zanubrutinib in their primary indications, extracts SAE incidence by category, and synthesizes a side-by-side safety comparison.

events.content: Compare the serious adverse event profile across the three approved BTK inhibitors. Group by event category and pivotal trial.
Three drugs, multiple pivotal trials each. I'll pull the head-to-head and pivotal trial publications, then extract SAE incidence by MedDRA SOC.

events.args: paperclip search "ibrutinib acalabrutinib zanubrutinib pivotal safety serious adverse event CLL" -n 20

events.content:
Found 14 documents  [s_btk]

  Sources: pmc(9), fda(3), trials/us(2)

  1. NEJM 2018 — RESONATE long-term (ibrutinib, r/r CLL)
  2. Lancet 2020 — ELEVATE-TN (acala, 1L CLL)
  3. JCO 2022 — ELEVATE-RR (acala vs ibrutinib, r/r CLL)
  4. NEJM 2023 — ALPINE (zanu vs ibrutinib, r/r CLL)
  5. Blood 2023 — SEQUOIA (zanu, 1L CLL)
  6. FDA Statistical Review — BLA 761085 (zanubrutinib CLL approval)
  7. JCO 2024 — ELEVATE-RR final analysis (acala vs ibru)
  8. Lancet Haematol 2024 — pooled BTKi cardiac safety MA
  ... [6 more]

events.args:
paperclip filter --from s_btk \
  "reports SAE incidence by category in a pivotal or head-to-head trial — exclude reviews and mechanism papers"

paperclip map --from s_btk_sae \
  "Extract: (1) trial, (2) drug + dose, (3) n, (4) any-grade and grade ≥ 3 incidence for: atrial fibrillation, hypertension, bleeding/hemorrhage, infection, cytopenias, headache. (5) discontinuation rate due to AE."

events.content:
Map complete: 8/8 documents  [m_btk_sae]

  ✓ RESONATE long-term (ibrutinib, r/r CLL, n=195)
    AFib: any 12%, ≥3 6%. HTN: any 23%, ≥3 8%. Major bleeding ≥3: 9%.
    Infection ≥3: 35%. Neutropenia ≥3: 22%. Headache: 21%.
    Discontinuation due to AE: 16%.

  ✓ ELEVATE-TN (acalabrutinib, 1L CLL, n=179 acala-mono)
    AFib: any 4%, ≥3 1%. HTN: any 4%, ≥3 2%. Major bleeding ≥3: 2%.
    Infection ≥3: 14%. Neutropenia ≥3: 11%. Headache: 37%.
    Discontinuation due to AE: 12%.

  ✓ ELEVATE-RR head-to-head final (acala vs ibru, r/r CLL)
    AFib (acala/ibru): any 9%/16%, ≥3 4%/9%. HTN: any 9%/23%, ≥3 4%/9%.
    Major bleeding ≥3 (acala/ibru): 5%/9%. Infection ≥3: 31%/31%.
    Headache: 35%/20%. Discontinuation due to AE: 14%/21%.

  ✓ ALPINE (zanu vs ibru, r/r CLL)
    AFib (zanu/ibru): any 5%/13%, ≥3 2%/4%. HTN: any 23%/22%, ≥3 15%/14%.
    Major bleeding ≥3: 3%/4%. Infection ≥3: 26%/27%.
    Discontinuation due to AE: 16%/23%.

  ✓ SEQUOIA (zanubrutinib, 1L CLL, n=241)
    AFib: any 3%, ≥3 0.7%. HTN: any 14%, ≥3 6%. Major bleeding ≥3: 3.8%.
    Infection ≥3: 16%. Neutropenia ≥3: 11%. Headache: 14%.
    Discontinuation due to AE: 8%.

  ✓ Pooled BTKi cardiac safety MA (Lancet Haematol 2024)
    Class-level AFib: ibru 12.5% > zanu 5.2% > acala 6.1%.
    Class-level HTN: ibru and zanu both elevated; acala lower.
    Major bleeding: similar across class once baseline
    antiplatelet use is adjusted for.

  ... [2 more aligned]

## BTK inhibitor SAE profile — side by side

Across the pivotal trials and the two head-to-head studies (ELEVATE-RR, ALPINE), a clear pattern has emerged: **ibrutinib carries higher cardiac and bleeding signal**, while **acalabrutinib and zanubrutinib show improved cardiovascular tolerability** with their own distinct profiles.

### Side-by-side SAE incidence (grade ≥ 3 unless noted)

| Category | Ibrutinib (RESONATE / ELEVATE-RR comparator / ALPINE comparator) | Acalabrutinib (ELEVATE-TN / ELEVATE-RR) | Zanubrutinib (SEQUOIA / ALPINE) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Atrial fibrillation | **6–9%** | **1–4%** | **0.7–2%** |
| Hypertension | **8–14%** | **2–4%** | **6–15%** |
| Major bleeding | **9%** | **2–5%** | **3–4%** |
| Infection | 31–35% | 14–31% | 16–27% |
| Neutropenia | ~22% | ~11% | ~11% |
| Headache (any-grade) | 20% | **35–37%** | 14% |
| Discontinuation due to AE | **16–23%** | 12–14% | 8–16% |

### What the head-to-head data show

- **ELEVATE-RR (acala vs ibru, r/r CLL)**: acalabrutinib better on AFib (4% vs 9%, grade ≥ 3), HTN (4% vs 9%), discontinuation (14% vs 21%). Worse on headache (35% vs 20%, mostly grade 1–2).
- **ALPINE (zanu vs ibru, r/r CLL)**: zanubrutinib better on AFib (2% vs 4%, grade ≥ 3) and discontinuation (16% vs 23%). HTN essentially tied — zanubrutinib does not have ibrutinib's AFib signal but inherits the HTN signal.

### Class pattern (Lancet Haematol 2024 meta-analysis)

- **AFib**: ibrutinib 12.5% > acalabrutinib 6.1% ≈ zanubrutinib 5.2%
- **HTN**: ibrutinib and zanubrutinib both elevated; acalabrutinib lower
- **Major bleeding**: similar across class after adjusting for baseline antiplatelet use

### Practical interpretation

1. **Ibrutinib should now be a second-choice agent** in any patient with cardiac risk factors. The head-to-head evidence is sufficient to recommend acalabrutinib or zanubrutinib as the default 1L BTKi in CLL.
2. **Acalabrutinib trades headache for cardiac tolerability.** Most headache is grade 1–2 and time-limited; cardiac AEs are not.
3. **Zanubrutinib has the lowest AFib rate** but maintains an ibrutinib-like HTN profile. Patients with pre-existing hypertension may do better on acalabrutinib.
4. **Infection rate is broadly similar across the class** — BTK pathway inhibition has consistent immune consequences regardless of which agent is used.

### Items worth confirming patient-by-patient

- Baseline antiplatelet / anticoagulant use (interaction with bleeding signal)
- History of arrhythmia or hypertension (drives drug choice)
- Concomitant CYP3A4 inhibitors (affects dosing for all three but particularly ibrutinib)
- Prior infection history and vaccination status

*Documents read: 11. Wall clock: 198 seconds.*
